question.text: Under the SEBI (Intermediaries) Regulations, 2008, what measures can the designated authority recommend after an enquiry?

answer.text: Cancellation or suspension of certificate of registration
Prohibition from taking new assignments or debarment of officers

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The designated authority may recommend: disposing of proceedings without adverse action, cancellation or suspension of registration, prohibition from new assignments, debarment of officers, debarment of branch offices, or issuance of regulatory censure. **The Trap:** Each individual option covers only part of the available measures - the full range is much broader. **Cross-Exam Context:** Disciplinary action measures are tested in NISM Series III-B exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Regulatory censure is the mildest action and is essentially a formal warning - but even this can damage reputation and business prospects.
